title: How to Speed Read People
author: David Lieberman
subline: Think like a psychologist, decode emotions.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A face split into a heatmap: most of it is a hairline outline, but 7 micro-zones (eyes, brow, mouth, jaw, cheeks) are highlighted with heatmap intensity — 6 in dim reds, 1 in BRIGHT RED (the one to watch). The viewer is being 'read'. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 950)" fill="none">
    <!-- Face outline -->
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="280" ry="380" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- Eyes -->
    <ellipse cx="-90" cy="-80" rx="40" ry="20" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <ellipse cx="90" cy="-80" rx="40" ry="20" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- Brow -->
    <line x1="-130" y1="-130" x2="-50" y2="-130" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
    <line x1="50" y1="-130" x2="130" y2="-130" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- Mouth -->
    <line x1="-60" y1="160" x2="60" y2="160" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- Nose -->
    <line x1="0" y1="-60" x2="0" y2="120" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- 7 micro-zones, marked with circular targets -->
    <g>
      <!-- EYES (1) — the strongest tell. Bright red. -->
      <circle cx="-90" cy="-80" r="46" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
      <circle cx="90" cy="-80" r="46" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
      <!-- BROW — dim red -->
      <circle cx="-90" cy="-130" r="22" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" opacity="0.4"/>
      <circle cx="90" cy="-130" r="22" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" opacity="0.4"/>
      <!-- MOUTH — dim red -->
      <circle cx="0" cy="160" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" opacity="0.3"/>
      <!-- JAW — dim red -->
      <circle cx="-180" cy="200" r="20" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" opacity="0.2"/>
      <circle cx="180" cy="200" r="20" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" opacity="0.2"/>
      <!-- CHEEKS — dim red -->
      <circle cx="-150" cy="60" r="18" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" opacity="0.15"/>
      <circle cx="150" cy="60" r="18" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" opacity="0.15"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Annotation lines pointing to the brightest target -->
    <line x1="-90" y1="-80" x2="-200" y2="-220" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.5"/>
    <text x="-380" y="-200" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">EYES</text>
    <text x="-380" y="-180" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="9" font-weight="400" fill="#fc1c46" opacity="0.6" letter-spacing="0.04em">— read these first</text>
  </g>
  <text x="800" y="380" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">FIG. 1 · THE 7 MICRO-EXPRESSIONS</text>
</svg>
rationale: A face split into a heatmap. 7 micro-zones are circled: the EYES are the only one in BRIGHT RED (the rest fade in opacity). 320px mixed-case 'How to / Speed Read People' title in weight 300 sits at the bottom. The visual thesis: 90% of the read is in the eyes; everything else is noise.
